Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Of Nonappearance
Off Calendar Aug-07-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Of Nonappearance.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted. The court takes judicial notice of its deemed-admitted order of May 6, 2025.

Wells Fargo brought a complaint claiming a breach of contract in defendant Abraham Luna's nonpayment of his credit card account. Wells Fargo claimed damages of $17,824.78. It obtained a deemed-admitted order conclusively establishing the truth of the matters specified in its requests for admission to which Luna did not respond. The matters deemed admitted are sufficient to establish plaintiff's entitlement to judgment in its favor on the breach of contract claim in that amount.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-17-2025 Continued To Aug-25-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Reqeusts For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance
Off Calendar May-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance

Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A., moves for an order that all matters specified in its requests for admission to defendant Abraham Luna be deemed admitted including the genuineness of attached documents. The motion is granted. Defendant failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs is deemed admitted by defendant. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).)

Plaintiff does not seek sanctions, but CCP 2033.280(c) provides, "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions of $50 payable by Abraham Luna to Wells Fargo Bank N.A. through its counsel. Luna shall remit sanctions within 30 days of entry of this order.

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
